definition: A 2-3x multiplier in inflation passthrough exists between anchored and de-anchored expectation regimes. The existence of this bimodality is the most empirically grounded finding across the knowledge base, supported by cross-country panel data across 40+ economies, though the regime boundary location remains unidentified.
evidence: Cross-country panel data across 40+ economies (Carrière-Swallow et al. 2016, Forbes 2019)
Replicated across sub-sample analysis (pre/post-GFC), alternative inflation measures, and different shock identification strategies
Both quant agents independently converge at confidence 9 — the only claim where this occurs
No agent challenged it; no debate weakened it
open_questions: Where is the regime boundary located in real time?
Can the boundary be identified with sufficient lead time to be actionable for portfolio positioning?
What observable indicators best predict proximity to the boundary?

definition: The probability of a wage-price spiral is low (<10-15%) under current conditions. Only 2 of ~16 post-war cycles produced true spirals, and current conditions satisfy 0 of 3-4 conjunctive prerequisites: union density, COLA coverage, sustained wage growth >5%, and expectations de-anchoring.
evidence: 2/~16 post-war cycles produced true spirals
Current conditions satisfy 0/3-4 conjunctive prerequisites
2021-23 natural experiment: 2/3 conditions temporarily met, no spiral materialized — genuine out-of-sample validation
Structural decline in union density and COLA coverage is secular, not cyclical
open_questions: Could novel labor market structures create spiral risk through channels not captured by the historical prerequisite set?

definition: The lagged deceleration of OER from ~5.0% to ~3.5-4.0% will deliver 40-80bp of mechanical headline CPI relief, creating a false 'mission accomplished' narrative window likely in Q2-Q4 2026. The 4/6 base rate for premature declarations during Phase 2 goods disinflation is the highest-conviction shared finding across all agents.
evidence: OER deceleration arithmetic: ~5.0% to ~3.5-4.0% delivers 40-80bp mechanical headline CPI relief
4/6 base rate for premature 'mission accomplished' during Phase 2 goods disinflation
1983 and 1995 precedents specifically involved shelter-led headline declines
Fed's revealed reaction function makes premature dovish signaling likely during this window
All four debate reports confirmed as highest-conviction shared finding
open_questions: Will services ex-shelter decelerate enough independently to prevent the trap?
Could productivity growth at 2.5%+ (as in the 1995 exception) validate the easing?
How will the Fed's 2025-2026 framework review interact with the masking window?

definition: The equity-bond correlation flips from negative (-0.3 to -0.4) to positive (+0.2 to +0.4) when inflation persistently exceeds ~2.5%, destroying 60/40 and risk parity diversification assumptions calibrated to the 2010-2020 covariance matrix. Identified as the single most consequential parameter change for multi-asset portfolios.
evidence: Academic literature: Ilmanen 2011, Campbell-Sunderam-Viceira updates
Post-GFC calibration assumed negative equity-bond correlation under core PCE ~1.5-1.8%
When inflation persistently exceeds 2.5%, correlation averages +0.2 to +0.4
pair_2 debate affirmed as 'the single most consequential parameter change' for multi-asset portfolios
open_questions: What is the exact inflation threshold for the flip? (sample-dependent)
How quickly does the correlation regime shift after inflation crosses the threshold?
Are risk parity and mean-variance portfolios calibrated to 2010-2020 structurally mispriced if the floor has shifted to 2.3-2.8%?

definition: Services inflation at 3.5-4.0% with strong unit labor cost support (3.0-4.0%) is the binding constraint on disinflation. Historical base rate of 0/5 (or 1/7 including 1994-95) for resolution without recession, though the Clopper-Pearson 95% CI of [0%, 52.2%] means the point estimate overstates certainty.
evidence: Services CPI at 3.5-4.0% with unit labor cost support of 3.0-4.0%
0/5 base rate for resolution without recession (1/7 including 1994-95 productivity-assisted success)
Clopper-Pearson 95% CI is [0%, 52.2%] — statistically honest framing
5 episodes are not i.i.d. draws from a stationary process
open_questions: Can immigration surge of 3-4M workers provide sufficient labor supply relief?
Can sectoral AI deployment create disinflationary pressure in CPI-relevant services categories?
Are there novel disinflationary channels with no analogue in the n=5 reference set?
What is the CPI weight of sub-categories where AI deployment is already live?

definition: Equity multiples (P/E ~20-21x) embed a disinflationary glide path, credit spreads (IG ~90-100bp, HY ~300-350bp) embed a soft landing, and the rates curve prices 3-4 cuts by year-end. These outcomes cannot all be correct simultaneously given services persistence findings.
evidence: Equity multiples embed disinflationary glide path
Credit spreads embed soft landing
Rates curve prices 3-4 cuts by year-end
generalist_02's financial conditions paradox validates from 4/5 historical episodes
All agents acknowledge the inconsistency; resolution direction is the disagreement
open_questions: Will the inconsistency resolve through inflation repricing upward or growth repricing downward?
Will credit or equity reprice first?
Does the financial conditions paradox delay resolution by 12-18 months?
Is the breakeven-specific component of the inconsistency weaker than originally claimed?

definition: Covenant-lite structures (>90% of leveraged loans) with 15-40% EBITDA addbacks delay default transmission by 12-24 months, converting gradual adjustment into clustered stress events through a delay-then-burst mechanism analogous to LTCM and SVB.
evidence: >90% covenant-lite is an observable market structure fact
15-40% EBITDA addback phenomenon overstates credit quality metrics
Delay-then-burst mechanism has structural analogues (LTCM, SVB — opacity creates information gaps resolving suddenly)
2020 episode too brief and rapidly reversed by policy to be fully informative
open_questions: What is the counterfactual Phillips Curve behavior without covenant-lite?
How much of the delay effect survives in market-implied distress measures (CDS-implied PD, option-adjusted spreads)?
When does the maturity wall force recognition?

definition: Fiscal deficits of 6-7% GDP are suppressing defaults by an estimated 100-150bp of spread equivalent, creating an unstable equilibrium. The channel's direction (deficits flow into corporate revenues and credit metrics) is uncontested; any material fiscal consolidation withdraws this support.
evidence: All four debate reports confirmed as load-bearing and fragile
Direction uncontested: fiscal deficits flow into corporate revenues and credit metrics
Unprecedented scale of deficit during non-recession, non-war disinflation attempt
No historical analogue fully validates the magnitude estimate
open_questions: What is the true fiscal multiplier (estimates range 0.5-1.5)?
How quickly would fiscal consolidation transmit to credit markets?
Is the 100-150bp magnitude estimate robust to alternative fiscal multiplier assumptions?

definition: The structural inflation floor has shifted upward due to housing shortage (3.5-5.5M units), deglobalization, and healthcare cost inflation. Direction is supported by multiple independent lines, but the honest confidence interval of approximately [-10bp, +100bp] includes zero when methodology uncertainty and correlated errors are incorporated.
evidence: Housing shortage is physical: 3.5-5.5M units
Deglobalization is observable
Healthcare cost inflation is structural
commodities_analyst_02 adds 5-10bp from agricultural structural factors
quant_researcher_01 demonstrates honest CI of [-10bp, +100bp] incorporating methodology uncertainty
Additive decomposition (housing 10-20bp + deglobalization 10-20bp + healthcare 10-15bp) implies false precision due to correlated component errors
open_questions: Are the component errors (housing, deglobalization, healthcare) correlated, and by how much?
Does the lower bound genuinely include zero?
What observable data would narrow the confidence interval?

definition: The Fed's reaction function exhibits a dovish asymmetry where recession costs are politically salient but 2.8% inflation is not. Qualitative existence is supported by political economy reasoning, but the specific magnitude (~200bp) is estimated from a single observation (September 2024 cut at core PCE ~2.7%) with zero degrees of freedom.
evidence: Political economy argument: recession cost is politically salient, 2.8% inflation is not
September 2024 cut at core PCE ~2.7% as single observation
Multi-cycle evidence shows asymmetry is not structural constant — absent under Greenspan 1994-95, reversed under Volcker
Two-threshold estimation from two observations is a just-identified system
open_questions: Will the 2025-2026 framework review shift the asymmetry?
How do FOMC composition changes affect the reaction function?
Is this a permanent structural feature or regime/personnel-dependent?

definition: Disinflation follows a three-phase sequence of goods, then shelter, then services. Pattern-matched from 6 episodes, useful as a descriptive heuristic for identifying sequencing patterns and risk windows, but not a precision timing model. The 4/6 trap rate is susceptible to hindsight bias without pre-registered predictions.
evidence: Pattern-matched from 6 historical episodes
Researcher degrees of freedom in defining phases, thresholds, and trap declarations
Heuristic value for identifying sequencing patterns confirmed across multiple agents
open_questions: Can the framework produce pre-registered predictions to avoid hindsight bias?
Does the current cycle follow the canonical sequence?
Can the framework time factor rotations or sector trades?

definition: RFS biofuel mandates create ~40% inelastic corn demand, meaning the residual 60% absorbs 100% of supply shocks, amplifying agricultural price moves by 1.5-2.5x. This is demand elasticity math applied to a legislatively observable fact, and is the strongest contribution from commodities analysis.
evidence: ~40% inelastic corn demand from RFS mandates is legislatively observable
Residual 60% absorbs 100% of supply shocks — clean demand elasticity arithmetic
1.5-2.5x price move amplification
Simultaneous multi-crop tightness across wheat/corn/soybeans impairs cross-substitution buffers
open_questions: Has the base probability of activation been confirmed by current WASDE data?
Would a specific weather event or triggered export ban provide confirming evidence for probability upgrade?
What is the conditional probability of multi-crop simultaneous tightness?

definition: Falling headline inflation loosens financial conditions while the underlying inflation regime is unresolved, extending credit cycles by 12-18 months. Documented across 4/5 historical episodes (1967-68, 1984-85, 1995-96, 2005-06). The 1995-96 exception required productivity growth at 2.5%+ to validate the loosening ex post.
evidence: 4/5 historical episodes show the pattern (1967-68, 1984-85, 1995-96, 2005-06)
Causal mechanism: falling headline → loosened conditions → extended credit cycle → delayed but amplified correction
1995-96 exception required productivity growth to validate loosening ex post
Directly challenges near-term credit repricing timing assumptions
open_questions: Is current productivity growth sufficient to validate loosening (2.5%+ threshold)?
How does the paradox interact with covenant-lite and fiscal support in the current cycle?
What is the magnitude of the eventual correction after the extended cycle?

definition: Fiscal consolidation risk is the single largest analytical gap in the KB framework. A fiscal impulse reversal from +1.5% GDP to -0.5% GDP would be a 2pp demand shock with no concept adequately modeling it. DOGE-driven spending reviews, debt ceiling dynamics, and reconciliation are real political vectors for this risk.
evidence: All four debate reports confirm as 'the single most important gap neither agent fully resolves'
DOGE-driven spending reviews, debt ceiling dynamics, and reconciliation are real political vectors
Functions as both credit risk (fiscal support withdrawal) and disinflationary channel (demand destruction)
Could short-circuit the sticky-inflation thesis from the demand side
open_questions: What is the probability and timing of material fiscal consolidation?
How would a 2pp demand shock interact with the sticky-inflation thesis?
What is the transmission lag from fiscal impulse reversal to credit markets?
Would fiscal consolidation resolve services inflation or create stagflationary dynamics?

definition: After 47 iterations, the KB framework exhibits confirmation bias with 20+ concepts and 15+ relationships, none articulating a disinflationary scenario. No falsification criteria are specified. The highest confidence scores all confirm the 'above-target but not spiraling' consensus. This is a structural critique the framework cannot answer by citing more evidence within itself.
evidence: Verifiable by inspection: zero concepts point toward faster-than-expected disinflation
No falsification criteria specified in the KB
pair_3 confirms: 'structural critique that Agent A cannot answer by citing more evidence within the existing framework'
The framework needs a disinflationary scenario branch regardless of whether the sticky-inflation thesis is correct
open_questions: What would a disinflationary scenario branch look like?
What are the appropriate falsification criteria for the sticky-inflation thesis?
Has the iterative process systematically filtered out disconfirming evidence?

definition: EBITDA addback practices (15-40%) in >90% covenant-lite loans systematically overstate reported EBITDA, meaning a firm reporting 4.0x leverage may be running 5.5-6.5x on a cash-flow-consistent basis. This corrupts accounting-based distress factors (Z-score, O-score, distance-to-default) at the input level.
evidence: >90% covenant-lite with 15-40% addbacks is observable market structure
Reported EBITDA systematically overstated as denominator in leverage and coverage ratios
pair_2 confirms as 'novel and important'
Corrupts accounting-based distress factors at the input level
open_questions: How much of the signal corruption survives in market-implied distress measures (CDS-implied PD, option-adjusted spreads)?
Can alternative cash-flow-consistent metrics replace corrupted accounting-based factors?
What is the aggregate magnitude of leverage understatement across the market?

definition: AI aggregate productivity gains will not resolve services inflation within 2026-2028, but sectoral deployment is already live in narrow CPI-relevant categories (customer service, coding, legal review). The gap between 'AI is deployed' and 'AI is disinflationary in CPI' — since firms may capture gains as margin — is the key unresolved question.
evidence: n=3 technology analogues under heterogeneous conditions cannot support confidence 8
Cloud-based software deployment compresses adoption vs. hardware-adoption templates
Both aggregate (won't move TFP) and sectoral (already deployed) can be simultaneously true
CPI impact depends on weight of affected sub-categories, which no agent quantifies
open_questions: What is the CPI weight of AI-affected sub-categories?
Will firms pass through AI productivity gains as lower prices or capture as margin?
What is the realistic timeline for aggregate TFP impact?
Does the software vs. hardware distinction compress the adoption timeline enough to matter for 2026-2028?

definition: The consensus 'sticky above-target' inflation view is the most crowded macro view per BofA Global Fund Manager Survey, with CFTC net short Treasuries near 2-year extremes and 5Y breakevens near 2.4-2.5%. A contrarian disinflation scenario (core PCE 2.3-2.5% for 2-3 months) offers a 3:1 to 5:1 payoff ratio because the consensus outcome is already priced.
evidence: BofA Global Fund Manager Survey: 'above-target inflation' is most crowded macro view
CFTC net short Treasuries near 2-year extremes
5Y breakevens near 2.4-2.5%
Contrarian scenario: 50-100bp Treasury rally vs. 10-20bp remaining return to consensus — 3:1 to 5:1 payoff ratio
pair_3 calls this 'the single most actionable insight'
open_questions: What is the probability of a contrarian disinflation scenario materializing?
How quickly would positioning unwind on 2-3 months of below-consensus prints?
What is the optimal structure for expressing this asymmetry?

definition: A compound analogue framework using multi-dimensional scoring is methodologically sounder than reliance on any single historical episode. No episode scores above 7.0/10 similarity due to four structural novelties: mortgage lock-in (85%+ below 5%), >90% covenant-lite, AI investment cycle, and simultaneous deglobalization + fiscal expansion at 6-7% GDP.
evidence: pair_0 judged compound approach stronger than single-analogue reliance
Four structural novelties limit any single analogue to <7.0/10 similarity
Seven-dimension scoring methodology provides systematic comparison
open_questions: Are the dimension weights in the scoring framework objective or subjective?
At what point does compound structural novelty invalidate analogue-based reasoning entirely?
Are outcome distributions wider than any analogue framework suggests?

definition: The threshold for agricultural export bans has shifted upward by an estimated 2-5pp in stocks-to-use terms due to costless defection behavior observed across India, Indonesia, Argentina, and Turkey during 2022-24, which collapses cooperation equilibrium at lower stress levels. WTO inability to discipline restrictions is factual.
evidence: Behavioral economics argument: costless defection collapses cooperation equilibrium at lower stress
2022-24 empirical record of export bans by India, Indonesia, Argentina, Turkey
WTO's inability to discipline export restrictions is factual
open_questions: Can the precise 2-5pp threshold shift be measured rather than estimated?
What stocks-to-use level triggers cascading bans?
How does the threshold interact with bilateral trade agreements?

definition: Developed market food CPI weights vary significantly — Japan 26%, EU periphery 18-22%, UK ~16%, US ~13.5% — creating cross-country policy divergence risk from agricultural supply shocks. The BOJ vulnerability (26% food weight + yen weakness) connecting to monetary normalization risk is an underappreciated channel.
evidence: CPI weights are published facts: Japan 26%, EU periphery 18-22%, UK ~16%, US ~13.5%
BOJ vulnerability from 26% food weight + yen weakness is a valid and underappreciated channel
open_questions: Would the agricultural shock need to materialize before this becomes a tradeable dislocation?
What is the cross-DM rates dislocation magnitude from a significant food price shock?
How does this interact with BOJ normalization timing?

definition: The Fed faces simultaneous Type I (premature easing from OER masking) and Type II (insufficient tightening from lagging indicators) policy error risk. These errors may be positively correlated: OER masking encourages premature easing (Type I), which simultaneously constitutes insufficient tightening (Type II) by definition.
evidence: Each error type individually well-documented in monetary policy literature
Compound probability may be elevated due to positive correlation between error types
Historical sample insufficient to estimate compound probability directly
open_questions: What is the compound probability of both errors occurring simultaneously?
Is the positive correlation between Type I and Type II errors a structural feature or coincidence?
What monitoring signals distinguish genuine disinflation from OER masking?

definition: The core PCE 'stall zone' of 2.5-3.5% has a historical duration of 18-36 months from goods disinflation completion, with a fat right tail to 48+ months. Markets pricing 12-month resolution are in the left tail. Equity markets rally during the stall zone in 4/5 analogues (median +18%), ending 3-6 months before resolution.
evidence: 5 episodes: median 24 months, mean 25 months, range 15-36+
Markets pricing left tail (12-month resolution)
4/5 analogues show equity rally during stall zone (median +18%), ending 3-6 months before resolution
open_questions: Could structural novelties compress or extend the stall zone duration?
What signals reliably indicate the stall zone is ending?
Is the equity rally pattern reliable enough to be actionable?
